title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have started their 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst since 2019.
Boston's new pitching acquisitions have struggled, with the starting rotation posting a 5.22 ERA in six games.
The team's defense has committed six errors in six games, maintaining last season's struggles with 116 errors.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault charges is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
Diggs pleaded not guilty to charges of felony strangulation and assault stemming from a December incident with his personal chef.
The New England Patriots expressed support for Diggs, who denies the allegations, citing a financial dispute with the chef.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police Sergeant Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while driving under the influence in a 2023 crash.
Middlesex DA's office was not notified by police about the crash details, leading to internal investigation by Judge Thomas Drechsler.
State and Woburn Police failed to report the fatal accident to the DA's office within the required 24-hour period.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, was sentenced to 15-20 years for fatally stabbing Jazreanna Sheppard after an online feud.
The stabbing occurred near the Park Street MBTA station in Boston on July 20, 2023.
Partsch had a previous violent crime conviction in 2017 for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Connecticut Sun WNBA franchise will relocate to Houston and rebrand as the Houston Comets.
NBA star Kevin Durant expressed support for the move, highlighting UConn as a consolation for Connecticut fans.
Former Celtics minority owner Steve Pagliuca's $325 million attempt to keep the Sun in Boston was blocked by the league.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Carlos Narvaez was scratched from the Red Sox lineup hours before the game against the Houston Astros.
Manager Alex Cora declined to elaborate on the reason for removing Narvaez, who was replaced by Connor Wong.
Narvaez confirmed the decision was not injury-related, while Wong maintained a .500 batting average this season.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots will exercise Christian Gonzalez's $18.1 million fifth-year option for the 2027 season, Coach Mike Vrabel announced.
Christian Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned a Pro Bowl nod and was a second-team All-Pro selection in 2024.
Gonzalez aims for a long-term extension despite previous injuries; he played 16 games in 2024 without missing playoffs.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored seven points in his last six games, including five goals and two assists.
The Boston Bruins won their fourth consecutive game with a 6-3 victory over the Dallas Stars.
David Pastrnak leads with 66 assists and 23 points in his last 14 games amid intense playoff preparations.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson of Everett is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson was inspired by the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and the city's response to trauma care needs.
She aims to honor trauma teams whose work advanced emergency care post-bombings.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a projected budget deficit of $48.4 million, driven by costs like snow removal and police overtime.
The city has implemented spending freezes and hiring delays to address the deficit before June 30 fiscal year end.
Boston Public Schools separately face a $53 million deficit, leading to a potential 400 positions cut next year.
